title: NOC 72420 – Oil and solid fuel heating mechanics | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Oil and Solid Fuel Heating Mechanics (NOC 72420) are the specialized technicians ensuring thermal comfort in regions where traditional natural gas infrastructure is absent. While Canada moves toward "Net-Zero," these professionals remain critical for the maintenance and high-efficiency conversion of oil, wood, and coal heating systems in rural, northern, and coastal communities. Classified as TEER 2, the role in 2026 has integrated digital combustion analysis and automated fuel-delivery monitoring to maximize the efficiency of aging systems and ensure compliance with stricter environmental emission standards.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group724 – Machine repair and maintenance trades

duties: System Layout: Reading and interpreting drawings or specifications to determine the layout of oil and solid-fuel heating systems.
Precision Installation: Laying out and assembling oil burner components, including thermostats, motors, and piping, and connecting them to fuel supplies and ventilation.
Testing & Calibration: Testing installed units and adjusting digital controls for proper functioning and optimal fuel-to-air ratios.
Diagnostics: Troubleshooting and repairing malfunctioning oil burners, wood boilers, and their integrated electronic controls.
Solid Fuel Maintenance: Installing, maintaining, and repairing coal and wood-burning heating systems, including chimney and venting inspections.
Preventative Service: Performing scheduled maintenance on high-efficiency burners to ensure 2026-grade environmental compliance.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school and a vocational program is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three- to four-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over four years of work experience and industry courses is required for trade certification.
Certification: Trade certification is compulsory in Nova Scotia and New Brunswick. It is voluntary but highly recommended in all other provinces.
Regulatory Oversight: In Ontario, certification from the Technical Standards and Safety Authority (TSSA) is required for "Oil Burner Technicians."
Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is the "Gold Standard," allowing you to work in any province and access high-paying government and industrial contracts.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate, with high stability in provinces that rely heavily on oil and wood heating:

Atlantic Canada (NS & NB): Strong Demand. Oil heating remains a primary heat source in the Maritimes. In Nova Scotia and New Brunswick, this is a compulsory trade, meaning certification is mandatory to practice.

Ontario: Moderate prospects. In February and March 2026, Ontario included NOC 72420 in targeted OINP invitations under the Skilled Trades and Foreign Worker streams. Minimum scores for regional draws (Eastern and Northern Ontario) started as low as 54, reflecting a need for technicians in rural service areas.
Quebec: Fair prospects. The median wage in Quebec is $28.00/hr, with roughly 88% of workers employed full-time. Demand is driven by the maintenance of existing residential wood and oil burners.
National Trend: While new installations are slowing due to heat-pump incentives, the maintenance and repair market is growing as homeowners look to extend the life of existing systems.
Why Work in Canada as a Heating Mechanic?
Essential Service Role: You provide a life-sustaining service in Canada’s harsh winters, particularly for remote populations.
High-Tech Efficiency: Gain experience with 2026-grade technologies, including bio-fuel burner conversions and high-efficiency solid-fuel gasification boilers.
Excellent Benefits & Stability: Approximately 90% of professionals in this NOC work full-time, and many receive full medical and pension benefits through major HVAC service companies.
Priority for PR: In early 2026, this NOC remains a target for Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) "Skilled Trades" draws and is eligible for the Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In February 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72420 with a score of 54+ (Northern/Eastern Ontario). This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.

Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Updated for 2026. Eligible candidates now require 12 months of full-time experience (up from 6 months) within the last 3 years to receive an ITA at lower CRS scores.
Saskatchewan / Alberta Trades Streams: These provinces offer fast-track routes for heating mechanics who have at least one year of local work experience.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Heating mechanics are classified as "High-Priority Essential Infrastructure Workers."
